data.title: Dish Defaults on Payments to Crown Castle Following Spectrum Rights Sale
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Crown Castle said it terminated its wireless infrastructure agreement with Dish Wireless after Dish defaulted on its payment obligations.

Dish, a 5G network provider owned by EchoStar, told Crown Castle and other partners in September 2025 that it was discontinuing its network business.

That same month, EchoStar said it would sell some of its spectrum rights to AT&T and SpaceX.

EchoStar sold the rights after the Federal Communications Commission threatened to strip some of its licenses, saying it wasn't effectively using the rights to compete with big wireless carriers.

Dish told Crown Castle in September it believed it was no longer required to honor remaining contractual obligations. While Dish continued making payments for some time, it recently failed to pay, Crown Castle said.

Crown Castle exercised its right to terminate the agreement and recover in excess of $3.5 billion in remaining payments owed. The Houston-based communications company said it doesn't expect the actions to impact full-year 2025 results.

Crown Castle said it supported EchoStar's sales to AT&T and SpaceX, but was committed to enforcing its rights under its agreement with Dish.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Alphabet Becomes Newest $4 Trillion Company, Joining Nvidia — WSJ
data.description:
By Katherine Blunt and Rolfe Winkler

Google parent Alphabet's value topped $4 trillion in intraday trade, making it the latest tech company to cross that threshold as investors reward the internet-search leader for its artificial-intelligence gains.

Shares in Alphabet rose 1.66% to a record $334.04 on Monday morning, putting its market capitalization at $4.03 trillion for a time. Its shares rose 65.4% last year.

Four technology companies have traded at market capitalizations of $4 trillion, and only Nvidia is still above that mark. Apple and Microsoft, both of which crossed the $4 trillion threshold last year, now have market capitalizations of $3.8 trillion and $3.6 trillion, respectively.

Google pulled well ahead in the race to develop AI with the recent launch of its Gemini 3 model, which earned praise for its speed, intelligence and creative capabilities. It outperformed competing models on more than a dozen benchmark tests scoring a range of intelligence categories. Gemini now has more than 650 million monthly users, up from 450 million last summer.

The model's success poses challenges for OpenAI, Anthropic and other AI startups. OpenAI's ChatGPT is still by far the most popular AI chatbot, and Anthropic's Claude is regarded as one of the best models for coding. But Gemini 3's advances have made it more capable for use in a wider range of applications.

Demonstrating Google's AI prowess, Apple on Monday announced that it had selected Gemini to power a more personalized version of its Siri chatbot coming later this year. Apple has struggled to develop AI tools of its own, and Siri's capabilities badly lag chatbots available from others.

Apple said it had entered a "multiyear collaboration" with Google to use Gemini as the foundation of its own AI models. "These models will help power future Apple Intelligence features, including a more personalized Siri coming this year," it said.

The rise in Alphabet shares last year marks a significant reversal in investor sentiment. The launch of ChatGPT in 2022 stoked fears at Alphabet that its Google search engine would lose significant traffic to chatbots, and the company was slow to launch competitive AI models.

During its annual developers conference in May, Google unveiled a series of sophisticated AI products and a revamped version of its search engine featuring AI Mode, which answers search queries in a chatbot-style conversation. Still, some investors doubted whether the new tools would be enough to give the company an edge against its competitors.

Then, in August, the debut of image-generation tool Nano Banana launched Gemini usage on its fastest trajectory to date, in part by appealing to younger users. The company said most of the growth has come from users between 18 and 34 years old.

The company then won a significant victory in September, when a federal judge declined to impose harsh penalties on the company after earlier finding it maintained an illegal monopoly in the search market. The judge said the AI race had made the search market more competitive already.

News Corp, owner of The Wall Street Journal, has commercial agreements to supply content on Google platforms and through Apple services.

Alphabet recently reported record quarterly revenue, largely because of growth in cloud computing and advertising. Its cloud business has become a core revenue driver, jumping 34% year over year.

Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway revealed on Nov. 14 that it had purchased 17.8 million shares of Alphabet during the third quarter, according to a securities filing. The shares were valued at more than $4.3 billion at the end of September.

Write to Katherine Blunt at katherine.blunt@wsj.com and Rolfe Winkler at Rolfe.Winkler@wsj.com

data.title: Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals Provides Corporate Update
data.description:
Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals International, a biopharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing novel therapies for diseases with unmet needs, has released its latest SEC 8-K filing. The report provides a comprehensive update on the company's financial performance, business operations, and strategic initiatives for the year 2025.

Financial Highlights

For the full year 2025, Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals reported net product revenue of $677.5 million, representing a significant year-over-year growth of approximately 62% compared to $417.0 million in 2024. The company's gross-to-net ratio improved to 8.4% from 9.8% in the previous year, influenced by the Inflation Reduction Act and prior period reserve adjustments.

The cash balance increased by $170.4 million, reaching $414.1 million by the end of 2025, with no debt reported. Kiniksa expects its current operating plan to remain cash flow positive on an annual basis.

Business and Operational Highlights

ARCALYST, Kiniksa's IL-1α and IL-1β cytokine trap, continues to gain traction as a preferred second-line treatment for recurrent pericarditis. By the end of 2025, approximately 18% of the 14,000 multiple-recurrence patients were actively on ARCALYST therapy. The average total duration of ARCALYST therapy is approaching three years, aligning with the median duration of the disease.

Since its launch, over 4,150 prescribers have written ARCALYST prescriptions for recurrent pericarditis. The company projects ARCALYST's net product revenue for 2026 to be between $900 million and $920 million.

Strategic Initiatives and Corporate Developments

Kiniksa is advancing its pipeline with several key initiatives. The company is conducting a Phase 2/3 clinical trial of KPL-387 in recurrent pericarditis, with data expected in the second half of 2026. Additionally, a Phase 1 first-in-human trial for KPL-1161, an Fc-modified monoclonal antibody IL-1 receptor antagonist, is planned to commence by the end of 2026.

Furthermore, Kiniksa's strong financial position supports these efforts and provides the ability to pursue additional value-creating opportunities.

Management's Perspective

Sanj K. Patel, Chairman and CEO of Kiniksa, highlighted the growing adoption of ARCALYST and the company's strategic focus on expanding its reach to first recurrence patients. Patel also emphasized the potential of KPL-387 to expand the IL-1α & IL-1β inhibition market through monthly self-administration with a liquid formulation.

Future Outlook

Kiniksa has provided guidance for 2026, expecting ARCALYST's net product revenue to be between $900 million and $920 million. The company anticipates data from the dose-focusing portion of the Phase 2 clinical trial of KPL-387 in the second half of 2026 and plans to initiate a Phase 1 trial for KPL-1161 by the end of 2026.

Original SEC Filing: Kiniksa Pharmaceuticals International, plc [ KNSA ] - 8-K - Jan. 12, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Gyrodyne Signs Second Amendment to Purchase and Sale Agreement With B2K Smithtown
data.description:
Gyrodyne entered into a Second Amendment to its Purchase and Sale Agreement with B2K Smithtown, updating terms for the Flowerfield property sale. The amendment provides a $1,520,222 purchase price credit at closing for specified on-site improvements to Lots 1 and 3, with B2K responsible for common facilities and offsite work. It confirms the investigation period has expired and sets a February 5, 2026 deadline to resolve certain third-party land use covenants, after which B2K may terminate by February 6, 2026 and recover its deposit if unresolved. Gyrodyne is assessing potential impacts on timeline and liquidation values.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Second Amendment to Purchase and Sale Agreement
  • Counterparty: B2K Smithtown
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 06 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Update sale terms and address title covenants

Original SEC Filing: Gyrodyne, LLC [ GYRO ] - 8-K - Jan. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Beyond Meat Signs Supplemental Indenture With Wilmington Trust
data.description:
Beyond Meat entered into a First Supplemental Indenture to modify its existing indenture governing the company’s Convertible Senior Secured Second Lien PIK Toggle Notes due 2030. The amendment adds a guarantee from Beyond Meat EU B.V., the company’s wholly owned subsidiary, and secures the Notes on a second-priority basis by the assets of the company and the subsidiary, subject to exceptions. The update is intended to strengthen the collateral and support for noteholders.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Supplemental indenture adding subsidiary guarantee for 2030 convertible senior secured second-lien PIK toggle notes
  • Counterparty: Wilmington Trust
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 12 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: Through 2030 note maturity
  • Reason: Enhance security package and creditor support

Original SEC Filing: BEYOND MEAT, INC. [ BYND ] - 8-K - Jan. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: AMC Entertainment Signs Supplemental Indenture With GLAS Trust Company
data.description:
AMC Entertainment entered into a Supplemental Indenture to amend the indenture governing Muvico’s Senior Secured Exchangeable Notes due 2030. The amendment updates the definition of the Exchange Rate and modifies Article IV-B(d)(i) to reflect agreed changes with noteholders. The supplemental agreement, executed with GLAS Trust Company as trustee and collateral agent, is intended to clarify mechanics under the notes and support the existing financing structure.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Supplemental indenture to Exchangeable Notes Indenture
  • Counterparty: GLAS Trust Company, as trustee and collateral agent
  • Signed / Effective: Jan 12 2026 / same
  • Reason: Update exchange rate definition and related covenant terms

Original SEC Filing: AMC ENTERTAINMENT HOLDINGS, INC. [ AMC ] - 8-K - Jan. 12, 2026
